questions.q: Which of the following is the primary function of the circulatory system?
Describe the structure and function of a capillary.
questions.answer: Capillaries are the smallest blood vessels in the body, with a diameter of about 7-10 micrometers. They have a thin wall with a single layer of endothelial cells.

questions.q: Describe the process of blood circulation in the body, including the pulmonary and systemic circuits.
questions.answer: Blood circulation in the body involves the continuous flow of blood from the heart to the lungs and then to the rest of the body, and back to the heart. The pulmonary circuit carries deoxygenated blood from the heart to the lungs and back to the heart. The systemic circuit carries oxygenated blood from the heart to the rest of the body and back to the heart.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The blood pressure in the pulmonary artery is __________ than the blood pressure in the aorta.
State the importance of the autonomic nervous system in regulating blood pressure.
questions.answer: The autonomic nervous system regulates blood pressure by controlling the diameter of blood vessels and the rate of heart contractions.

questions.q: What is the function of the lymphatic system in relation to the circulatory system?
questions.answer: The lymphatic system helps to remove excess fluid and proteins from the tissues and returns them to the bloodstream.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a function of the circulatory system?
Describe the structure and function of a blood vessel.
questions.answer: Blood vessels are the tubes through which blood flows. They are made up of three layers: the tunica intima, the tunica media, and the tunica externa. The tunica intima is the innermost layer, the tunica media is the middle layer, and the tunica externa is the outermost layer.

questions.q: What is the difference between deoxygenated blood and oxygenated blood?
questions.answer: Deoxygenated blood is the blood that has released its oxygen to the tissues and is returning to the heart, while oxygenated blood is the blood that has picked up oxygen from the lungs and is being carried to the tissues.

questions.q: True or False: The heart is a muscular pump that circulates blood throughout the body.
